summary: Kenneth Suna discusses building a diversified stock portfolio by selecting the largest holdings from 11 sector-specific ETFs as a research starting point. The content focuses on stock picking methodology and portfolio diversification principles, not options trading strategies. This is fundamental investing education unrelated to Brian Norton's Premium Desk covered call and cash-secured put wheel strategy.
takeaways: 11 sectors exist in the market and can each be represented by sector-specific ETFs
Review top 10 holdings and specifically largest holdings of sector ETFs as research starting points for individual stock selection
Monitor sector ETF holdings regularly as fund rebalancing can shift which stocks are the largest holdings year-to-year
Individual stocks held alongside broad ETFs can offer potential for disproportionately higher returns versus ETF-only approach
title: Is this the most diverse portfolio EVER??
